query: Use /demo to turn this fictional agent skill into a realistic local try-it experience and give me the verified preview.
/demo revise my existing harbor-briefing demo in place and preserve its hand-authored field note.
Invoke the demo skill for this browser-helper concept; stage the host page and focal action without implementing an extension.
Please use demo as the named workflow to make this artifact tryable under ~/.demo and verify it on loopback.
Run /demo on the attached onboarding checklist and make completion plus reset the focal interaction.
Activate the demo skill and build the smallest convincing local experience for this release artifact.
/demo update the named amber-signal demo, test its main control path, and return restart and QA evidence.
I explicitly want the demo skill: create a staged chat simulator for this agent workflow with no live model backend.
Use /demo to mock the missing product context around this artifact, label simulated behavior, and keep everything local.
Start the demo workflow for these two candidate artifacts; ask one question if choosing between them changes the experience.
Build a demo page inside this existing Next.js app and add it to the product router.
Open our current localhost demo in a browser and test the checkout button at mobile width.
Create a throwaway prototype to compare these two state models before we implement either one.
Turn this Markdown into a durable single-file HTML artifact and serve it locally.
Write a five-minute sales demo script with speaker notes and likely prospect questions.
Review this demo repository for bugs and explain why the reset control is broken; do not edit anything.
Serve the existing dist folder on 127.0.0.1 so I can inspect the app that is already built.
Design three radically different UI directions for the onboarding flow and compare their tradeoffs.
Make an animated product-demo video from these screenshots and add captions.
Explain how to create safe deterministic mocks for a live API during frontend development.
